CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
P.O. BOX I76E, NEWPORT BEACH, CA 92659.1768

Sacranento, CA 9 5814
Re3 Request for Formal written Advice
John w. Hedges, Menber of the Newport Beach City Council
Dear I.{r. Hallabrin 3
This letter is a request for formal- written advice pursuant to
Section 83114(b) of the Governnent Code and Section 18329 of the
Regulations of the Fair Political- Practices Comrnission. I have
been authorized by John w. Hedges, a member of the Nelrport Beach
City Council, to make this request on his behalf and the request is
within rny duties as specified in the Newport Beach City Charter.
This request contains all, materj.al- facts known to Mr. Hedges and
me. In addition, f have contacted two real- estate appraisers who
specialize in valuingr property in the area relevant to this inquiry
and their comments are made a part of this request.
John Hedges is currently a member of the Nev/port Beach city
counci]. Mr. Hedges was elected to this office in Novenber, 1990.
The Newport Beach Cj-ty Charter provides that Councilmembers are
nominated frorn, and run for office as representative of, one of
seven districts, 
but are el-ected at Iarge, At a1l- times relevant
to this request, Mr. Hedges has been a resident and representative
of the First Councilrnanic District. 
The First 
Councilrnanic
District incl,udes the Bal,boa Peninsul,a and Lido IsIe.
Mr. Hedges's principal resi-dence is 2134 Miramar, Balboa,
( I'Res j,dencerr ) and he also owns property at 2914 west Oceanfront,
Newport Beach (rrRental Houserr). The Rental House is a two-story
duplex on a 25 foot by 90 foot Iot. 
The structure consists of
approximately 3,000 square feet of floor area. The Rental House
was purchased for $460,000 in 1986 and is currently valued at
approximately $f.05 million doIIars. The Renta1 House is Leased on
a Winter/Summer basis. 
During the Summer, the Renta} House is
leased on a weekly basis at $3,000 per week. Bet$/een Sumrners, the
Rental House is feased on a ]ong term basis with the exception of
one week when it is kept vacant for repairs and rnaintenance.
The Rental House j-s irnrnediately adjacent to a 12 foot sidewalk
comrnonly known as the oceanfront Boardwal,k or oceanfront WaIk
( t'Boardwalktt) . The Residence is approximately 1500 feet from the
Boardwalk. 
The Boardwal,k is heavily used by pedestrians,
{-L
rrJ
3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach


scott Halfabrin, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Comrnission
March 16, l-99 2
Page 2.
bicyclists, 
skaters and skateboarders. 
The Boardwal-k was
constructed in 1930 and presently accommodates as rnany as 600 to
800 users per hour.
In August, 1991-, an Orange County jury awarded $27O,OOO to a
person who, while walking on the Boardwal-k, suffered a broken leg
when struck by a bicyclist 
This award and two l-arge claims which
have been filed since August have sparked renewed interest in
widening the Boardwal-k and/or constructing a separate bicycle
facility 
further out onto the beach. The Boardrn/a1k extends for
more than 2-f/2 miLes and in some areas, the beach is 5oO feet or
more in width. However, the beach is probably not v/ide enough in
the area near the Rentaf House to accornmodate a separate bicycle
facil ity.
I have consulted wj-th two MAI appraisers fami]iar with the
val,ue of property in this area and have given them the inforrnation
contained in this l-etter. 
One appraiser believes there is no way
to determine if the construction of a separate bike path or wider
Boardwalk would have any irnpact on the value of the rentat house.
The second appraiser believes that the inpact on val,ue coul,d be
deterrnined, that the analysis of impact v/oul,d be sornewhat complex
and that a wider sidewa1k coutd slightly diminish the value of the
Rental House. Both appraisers \4rere convinced that changes in the
speed lirnit on the Boardwalk, the introduction of walk zones, and
other measures to control traffic would have no impact on the value
of any property along the Boardr,/alk.
There are approximatel-y 320 residentiaL properties sirnifar to
the Rental House that are adjacent to the Boardwal-k. There are
approximately 55 comnercial parcels and a multi-unit condoniniurn
development also adjacent to the Boardr^ra1k.
The City Councif has appointed a committee to study ways to
reduce the risk of City liability 
for injuries sustained by
Boardwalk users. 
As a natter of practice, the Councilrnember
representing any district invofved in such study v/ou1d be appointed
to the committee as a chairperson. 
Councilmenber Hedges was
designated the chairperson of that conmittee. 
The committee
divided its task into two phases--the first 
being short term
measures to improve the flow of traffic 
and improve user safety.
The cornrnitteers recommendations including the adoption of speed
Iimits, the implernentation of !/afk zones, and revisions to traific
flor^r, were approved by the City Council in January. The comnittee
is prepared to embark upon the second phase of its charge--the
developrnent of various long tern measures such as the construction


of a separate bike trail, 
the.widening of the existing facility,
and a sl-ight oceanward rel,ocation of the existing aoard-wal-k with-a
sand landscape buffer betv/een the landscape and residential
parcels.
The city Council" comrnittee will spend approxirnately 6 months
analyzing alternatives. 
The committee will Lhen prepari a report
to the City council which wilf contain recommenditj,ons . 
The
Council will then determine if it wants to process any of the
afternatives after re_cei.ving input from residents and 6onmunity
leaders. 
The councir wiff 
cond.uct more than a "siqnificant
substantj-ve reviewrr of commj,ttee recommendat ions . , Any proposa]
to widen the Boardwalk or construct a separate bike -p;th dill
require amendrnents to the Lcp and General- pLan, as well as
preparation of an EIR.
Mr. Hedges woul"d like formal written advice on the following:
1. 
Does he have a financiar interest in any decision to v/iden the
Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path
oceanward of 
the 
RentaL House that 
would reqiire
disgual i fication pursuant to the po]itical Reform Act?
2. 
Assuming Mr. Hedges. has a disqualifying economic interest,
wou]d Mr. Hedgesr involvement with the Council committee
designated to study this issue constitute participation in the
naking of a governrnental decision as tha€ term is defined in
the Act?
Scott Hal-l-abrin, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 16, 1992
Page 3.

cc: Councilmernber John Hedges

th" counci t corlrnissioned a similar study in 1986 and received a report recofimending the
construction of a separate.bile Path in certain areas and a rider Boardtatk in the area near the Rental House.
lhe consul,tant's recomEndations xere vehenentty opposed by a large nurber of persons [iving on or near the
Boardxalk. The counci [ .ejected the consuttantrs recofinerdations al a resutt or'ttis potitlcai op[sition ana

Robert H. Burnhan
City Attorney

sections.question:
1. Does he have a financial interest in any decision to widen the Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path oceanward of the Rental House that would require disqualification pursuant to the political Reform Act?

2. Assuming Mr. Hedges has a disqualifying economic interest, would Mr. Hedges's involvement with the Council committee designated to study this issue constitute participation in the making of a governmental decision as that term is defined in the Act?
sections.question_synthetic: Does Newport Beach City Councilmember John Hedges have a disqualifying financial interest under the Political Reform Act regarding potential decisions to widen the adjacent Boardwalk or construct a separate bike path near his rental property, and if so, would his committee involvement constitute prohibited participation in such governmental decisions?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion/advice section present. Document contains detailed factual background about the rental property, its valuation, the Boardwalk situation, and the committee's work. Two specific questions posed by requestor are clearly delineated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Does he have a financial interest in any decision to widen the Boardwalk adjacent to, or construct a separate bike path oceanward of the Rental House that would require disqualification pursuant to the political Reform Act?

2. Assuming Mr. Hedges has a disqualifying economic interest, would Mr. Hedges's involvement with the Council committee designated to study this issue constitute participation in the making of a governmental decision as that term is defined in the Act?

embedding.summary: City Attorney of Newport Beach requests formal written advice on behalf of Councilmember John Hedges regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from his ownership of a rental property adjacent to the Oceanfront Boardwalk and his appointment as chairperson of a committee studying improvements to the Boardwalk.
